classes.purpose: Find and interpret prior studio documents within an explicit archive scope
classes.content: User questions; the configured absolute archive-root path; scope names; relative document paths; search snippets; opened source passages; file, page, paragraph, sheet-row, message-line, or text-line locators; source hashes; extraction limitations; and Vera's source-backed synthesis

classes.purpose: Find and interpret Gmail correspondence for one explicitly selected studio client
classes.content: Confirmed client email or PEC addresses, legal names and tax identifiers; the exact archive scope; bounded Gmail queries; connector account profile; matching message and thread identifiers; sender and recipient headers; subjects, timestamps, snippets and labels; selected message or thread bodies; selected attachment metadata or content; exact-address routing evidence; ambiguity findings; search coverage; and Vera's source-backed synthesis

boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: User-selected Gmail account through the Codex Gmail connector
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Search and read correspondence for one explicitly selected client scope when the user chooses Gmail evidence
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: Bounded Gmail-native queries derived from the selected scope's confirmed identities and the user's topic or date bounds; Gmail returns message metadata and snippets first, followed only by shortlisted bodies, thread context, or supported attachment content that may enter Codex context
controls: Use Gmail only after the user explicitly requests or chooses mailbox search for the selected client; that route choice is the confirmation.
Call the connector profile action before search because the local Studio Archive cannot inspect or enforce which Gmail account the connector selected.
Use only Gmail search and read actions; never send, draft, forward, archive, delete, label, move, or otherwise mutate mailbox state.
Plan one exact client scope, shortlist before reading bodies or threads, and read an attachment only after its parent message is routed and the connector marks that attachment as supported.
Route automatically only after a full message read exposes all available From, To, Cc, and Bcc fields and every non-empty value parses; incomplete or unparseable header coverage fails closed.
The Gmail connector owns authentication and account selection; Vera does not request or store credentials, tokens, cookies, message bodies, attachments, or a mailbox copy.

security_controls.control: Each professional keeps configuration and the derived SQLite index in an owner-only local state directory outside the source archive; the workflow does not provide index sharing.

Search requires an exact configured top-level scope, symbolic links are skipped or rejected, relative paths are normalized, source and state roots cannot contain one another, and bounded discovery rejects unsafe scale.
Each searchable chunk is bound to the source SHA-256, relative path, and locator; opening a citation re-hashes the current source and fails closed after byte changes.
OCR is optional, uses Vera's local adapter with model downloads disabled, and reports unavailable weights or partial extraction rather than using an external OCR route.

Confirmed full email or PEC addresses, legal names, and tax identifiers are stored in an owner-only local registry outside the shared archive; the registry schema has no fields for Gmail credentials, tokens, cookies, message identifiers, bodies, or attachments.

Only one unique confirmed full-address match with complete, parseable From, To, Cc, and Bcc coverage can route a Gmail message automatically; zero-client, multi-client, incomplete, and unparseable results fail closed, legal names and message meaning require model review, studio-wide Gmail planning is rejected, and orphaned profiles can move only through an explicit one-to-one rebind.
